name: sim_weyl_pairwise_g2_bc2_coupling
scope_note: Pairwise coupling probe: W(G2) <-> W(B2). Step 2 of coupling program. W(G2)=Dih6 (order 12) and W(B2)=Dih4 (order 8) are non-isomorphic; Coxeter orders differ (6 vs 4); G2 A21=-3 vs B2 A21=-2 (distinct length ratios). Cross-group reflections do not commute; G2 long-root reflections escape B2 root set. G2 and B2 share +-e1 (2 roots); combined union = 18 distinct roots. z3 UNSAT: 12==8 arithmetic impossibility.

pytorch.reason: Represent all 12 G2 roots and 8 B2 roots as 2D float64 tensors; apply cross-group reflections; compute ||s_G2(s_B2(v)) - s_B2(s_G2(v))|| as the non-commutativity measure; verify G2 reflections escape B2 root set

pyg.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

z3.reason: UNSAT: |W(G2)| = |W(B2)| — encode 12 == 8 as Z3 integer constraint; direct arithmetic contradiction makes order-equality structurally impossible

cvc5.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

sympy.reason: G2 Cartan matrix [[2,-1],[-3,2]] and B2 Cartan [[2,-1],[-2,2]]; verify Coxeter orders symbolically: (s1*s2)^6=I for G2, (s*t)^4=I for B2; confirm A21=-3 (G2) != -2 (B2): structurally distinct root length asymmetries

clifford.reason: G2 and B2 roots in Cl(2,0); cross-system Clifford reflections s_G2(v) = -alpha_G2 * v * alpha_G2^{-1} and s_B2(v) = -beta_B2 * v * beta_B2^{-1}; verify they produce distinct outputs: cross-system Clifford interference

geomstats.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

e3nn.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

rustworkx.reason: Cayley graph of W(G2) (12-node dodecagonal pattern) vs W(B2) (8-node Dih4); compare node counts and edge structure; verify distinct graph topology; cross-group edges record non-commuting generator pairs

xgi.reason: Cross-group hyperedge {s_G2_reflection, s_B2_reflection, non_commutativity_flag}: 3-way relationship between the two reflection generators and their non-commutativity; encodes the G2+B2 coupling as a higher-order hyperedge structure

toponetx.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

gudhi.reason: not used in this Weyl geometry probe; deferred to integration sims

P1_pytorch_group_orders_differ.reason: W(G2)=Dih6 has order 12; W(B2)=Dih4 has order 8; different orders => non-isomorphic

P2_pytorch_root_counts_and_intersection.reason: G2 has 12 roots; B2 has 8 roots; they share +-e1 (2 roots) as their common short root; combined union has 18 distinct roots

P3_pytorch_noncommutativity_cross_group.reason: ||s_G2(s_B2(v)) - s_B2(s_G2(v))|| > 0: cross-group reflections do not commute

P4_pytorch_g2_long_root_reflection_escap.reason: G2 long-root reflection (alpha2=(-3/2,sqrt(3)/2)) maps some B2 roots outside B2 root set: hexagonal G2 symmetry escapes square-type B2 root geometry

P5_pytorch_reflection_counts_differ.reason: G2 has 6 hyperplane reflections (det=-1 elements); B2 has 4; distinct reflection structure

P6_pytorch_combined_orbit_bounded.reason: Combined orbit of generic vector under W(G2) x W(B2) has at most 96 (=12*8) images

P7_sympy_cartan_matrices_differ.reason: Cartan(G2)[2,1]=-3 vs Cartan(B2)[2,1]=-2; encodes long/short ratio sqrt(3) vs sqrt(2)

P8_sympy_coxeter_orders_differ_6_vs_4.reason: G2 Coxeter order = 6 ((s1*s2)^6=I); B2 Coxeter order = 4 ((s*t)^4=I); structurally distinct

P9_clifford_cross_system_reflections_dif.reason: G2 alpha2 and B2 beta2 are distinct roots; their Clifford reflections of same vector differ

P10_rustworkx_distinct_cayley_subgraph_s.reason: W(G2) Cayley graph: 12 nodes (Dih6); W(B2): 8 nodes (Dih4); structurally distinct

N1_z3_unsat_group_orders_equal.reason: UNSAT: |W(G2)|=12 AND |W(B2)|=8 AND 12==8 is a direct arithmetic contradiction

N2_pytorch_g2_rotation_not_in_b2.reason: The 60-degree rotation of W(G2) is NOT in W(B2) (Dih4 has only 90-degree rotations): non-inclusion

N3_pytorch_b2_elements_not_in_g2.reason: Some W(B2) elements (e.g., 90-degree rotation) are not in W(G2): non-inclusion

N4_sympy_g2_not_b2_coxeter_b2_not_order2.reason: G2 does NOT satisfy (s*t)^4=I (its order is 6); B2 minimal Coxeter order is 4 (not 2)

N5_clifford_g2_b2_long_roots_differ.reason: G2 alpha2 and B2 beta2 are distinct vectors; their Clifford reflections of same vector differ

N6_rustworkx_cayley_graphs_not_isomorphi.reason: Cayley graph node counts differ (12 vs 8): graphs cannot be isomorphic

B1_pytorch_identity_shared_fixed_point.reason: Identity element I commutes with all elements of both W(G2) and W(B2): shared boundary

B2_pytorch_zero_vector_fixed_by_all.reason: Zero vector is fixed by all Weyl group elements from both G2 and B2: degenerate boundary

B3_pytorch_shared_alpha1_beta1_reflectio.reason: Both G2 s1 and B2 sb1 map e1 -> -e1: shared A1 = Z2 subgroup (alpha1=beta1=e1)

B4_sympy_combined_cartan_block_diagonal.reason: G2+B2 combined Cartan = block diagonal; rank 4; off-diagonal blocks zero = no direct root coupling

B5_z3_sat_shared_alpha1_reflection_agree.reason: SAT: both groups share s_alpha1=s_beta1 (same root e1); they agree on all vectors at this boundary

B6_clifford_scalar_commutes_with_all.reason: Clifford grade-0 scalar commutes with all elements: boundary condition at the identity

B7_xgi_boundary_shared_a1_hyperedge.reason: XGI: shared A1 boundary links G2 and B2; divergence to G2 and B2 from shared Z2 subgroup
